<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The cell directly in front of me at row 02 column C is lava (`L`), which is deadly. Therefore, I cannot move forward. To avoid lava and continue towards the goal at row 04 column D, I need to change my direction. Looking at the map, turning downwards (`v`) would allow me to go around the lava safely. From facing right (`>`), turning clockwise will make me face down.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am at cell row 02 column B and I am facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Moving forward would put me on lava at row 03 column B, which is not safe. I should turn right to face left (`<`) or left to face right (`>`). From facing down, turning clockwise will make me face left (`<`) but that is a wall, so it is not safe. Turning counter-clockwise will make me face right (`>`), which is a safe floor cell at row 02 column C. So I will turn counter-clockwise first to face right.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>

To reach it safely, I can rotate downwards by turning clockwise twice to face down (`v`), then move forward safely in subsequent steps.
Thus, my first action is to turn 90 degrees clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO

The better path is to turn left to face right (`>`), then move forward along safe floor cells toward the goal.
Therefore, I will turn clockwise to face right.

GIRA_HORARIO
